pages.raw_text:
City Engineer Mccartney has
set in operation steam drill to
expedite the blasting work for
piers and abutments of the Mon
roe street bridge.


Nearly 5000 voters have regis
tered so far.


Many prominent Masons were
present at the trial of Thomas
Perrot, charged with selling fake
| Masonic degrees. which was con-
tinued in the police court yester-
day, and will probably be conclud-
ed today.


The members of the city council
held a brief meeting last night but
took no action on the city hall site
question.


r. 3. Irwin, of Cleveland, who is
totally blind. is in the city trying
to interest people in better legis-
lation for the blind of the state.


A. G. Bourret, reported as miss
ing from his hotel by his fiancee,
Miss Anna Good, of Standpoint,
has returned to his sweetheart.
franks will be legal after August
II, except those issued to certain
prescribed persons. chiefly officers
of telegraph, telephone and cable
companies.


Charged with deserving his wife
in Enaville. Idaho, Stanley John
son, alias T. w. Jones. was ar
rested at the Spokane hotel last
night by Sergeant Warner.


The police are looking for two
men, DIR" Carl Wittenberg and
Charles Whitson. who, allege citi-
zens of Medical Lake, have been
illtreating a young woman. Miss
Ethelyn Thomas, of ST Paul. The
trio have been staying at Medical
Lake together for the past fort
night. but it is believed that they
are now in Spokane.


Mrs. NJ J. Reynolds, equal suf-
frage speaker, has the following
engagements: Tonight in the
grange hall at Orchard avenue;
Saturday afternoon at the good
government rally at Mantle park;
Sunday morning at II, at the res
cue mission; Sunday night, in the
Christian church at Medical Lake;
Monday evening In the Baptist
church at Latah; Thursday even
ing, in the Christian church at
Pullman; Saturday evening, Aug
ust 20, Congregational church,
Five Mile prairie.


Carl S. Evans and George G.
Cody have formed partnership
as commercial printers and opened
up a shop at Thirteenth and Chest
nut streets in Peaceful valley.
Word from Palouse is to the ef-
feet that the wheat harvest is
coming on fine and that the crop
of fall wheat will average close TO
